description: At the end of 'pelio' (arm), 'manuwe' grasps, touches, gives, and works through its fingers. 'thumai' (finger) is one of those articulated digits rather than the hand as a whole.
articulatory_notes: Close the lips for voiced 'm', send the sound through the nose, and open into central unrounded 'a'. Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for dental 'n', maintain nasal airflow, and release into stressed high-back rounded 'u'. Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for voiced 'w', then move forward and unround into middle-height front 'e'.
usage_notes: The possessor precedes 'manuwe'. In descriptions of touch, the clause can name the hand, the person touched, and whether the contact was requested.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: A hand acquires craft knowledge through repeated contact with tools and material, knowledge that explanation alone cannot supply.

semantic_domains.creation: the body part through which much manual craft is performed
semantic_domains.physical: the grasping and touching part at the end of an arm
